query: Is there really nothing you can do? My wife just passed away yesterday, and I can’t travel on those dates. I’m really struggling right now. Isn’t there any way to make an exception? I don’t want to cancel, just change the date.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user asks to change the date of a flight because of a serious personal emergency.
assistant: The assistant retrieves reservation EUJUY6 and explains that it is a basic-economy round trip, so ordinary self-service date changes are not available under the visible policy context.
user: The user shares a bereavement situation, asks whether any exception is possible, and says they still want to change rather than cancel the trip.

candidate_answers.text: I’m very sorry for your loss. Because the visible booking details suggest this cannot be handled through the normal self-service flow, the next grounded step is to connect you with a human agent who can review exception options.

The user is requesting a policy exception after the reservation details have already been checked, so escalation is more appropriate than retrying the same reservation lookup or making an unsupported self-service change.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a user turn where a policy-blocked self-service change request turns into an exception appeal tied to a hardship circumstance, making human review the most grounded next action.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible state. Highest score goes to escalation because the booking has already been checked and the remaining request is an exception outside normal self-service handling.
notes: query comes directly from the selected visible user turn
history_summary stops before the assistant transfers the case to a human agent
best_action is the argmax because visible context no longer supports a standard automated update_reservation_flights action
this airline sample is included only for diversity after prioritizing retail and telecom cases
